items.q: Where is almost all the mass of an atom located?
items.correct: In the nucleus, made up of protons and neutrons.
items.misconception: Electrons contribute significantly to the mass of an atom.
items.random_wrong: Mass is spread evenly throughout the entire atom including the electron shells.
items.why: Students see electrons listed alongside protons and neutrons and assume they contribute comparable mass — in reality an electron has ~1/1836 the mass of a proton.

items.q: Why does water have a bent shape rather than linear?
items.correct: Oxygen has two bonding pairs and two lone pairs, giving a tetrahedral electron geometry but a bent molecular shape.
items.misconception: Water is bent because the hydrogens repel each other and push apart.
items.random_wrong: Water is bent because oxygen is heavier than hydrogen and weighs the middle down.
items.why: Students see H atoms at the ends and intuit H-H repulsion, missing that the bend is caused by lone pair-bond pair repulsion on the oxygen.

items.q: How does a buffer maintain its pH when small amounts of acid are added?
items.correct: The conjugate base in the buffer reacts with added H+ to remove it, keeping pH nearly constant.
items.misconception: A buffer prevents any H+ ions from being added to the solution at all.
items.random_wrong: A buffer destroys added acid by changing it into water vapour.
items.why: Students treat 'buffer' as a magical pH-locker rather than a chemical equilibrium that absorbs H+ or OH- through reactions with its components.
Correct answer: Melts at a sharp temperature. “Melts over a wide range” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Why is the chromatography baseline drawn in pencil?
items.correct: Ink would dissolve and run up the paper
items.why: Correct answer: Ink would dissolve and run up the paper. “Pencil is easier to see” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A chromatogram shows 4 distinct spots from one sample. The sample must contain at least how many substances?
items.why: The real figure is 4. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
